title: Ambulance Technician
how_to_become: You can become an ambulance technician by applying for training opportunities on the NHS recruitment website. A good standard of education and a full UK driving licence including C1 classification are required.

salary.entry_note: Qualified ambulance technicians start at £34,544 before tax. Trainees are paid £30,127, increasing as they progress through the training programme.
salary.experienced_note: Ambulance technicians can earn up to £43,039 per year before tax.

description: Ambulance technicians work with paramedics to deliver high-quality pre-hospital care. They provide a front-line response to accidents and emergency incidents including 999 calls, providing immediate care to people in very difficult and challenging environments.
role_overview: As an ambulance technician, you'll work with qualified paramedics to deliver the highest possible level of care. Patient needs and emergency situations are different every day, requiring flexibility, compassion, and professionalism in a range of environments and with a range of people.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education and a full UK driving licence including C1 classification are required.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.training_programme: A 16-month internal Scottish Ambulance Service education programme. Trainees are employees from their start date.
training_includes: 4-week Certificate in Emergency Response Ambulance Driving
qualifications_required: Level 4 Diploma for Associate Ambulance Practitioners (equivalent to SCQF level 7)
Certificate in Emergency Response Ambulance Driving at SCQF level 6
education_and_training.note: Once training is complete, you'll be allocated to an ambulance station with ongoing learning and support from qualified staff.

learning_and_development.overview: You must keep your skills and knowledge up to date throughout your career. The Scottish Ambulance Service will provide training specific to your role.

career_progression.overview: Ambulance technicians may progress to become a paramedic by completing an undergraduate degree in Paramedic Science.
degree_universities: University of the West of Scotland

professional_bodies: UK Registry of Emergency Medical Technicians (voluntary)

chunks.text: To become an ambulance technician, apply for training opportunities on the NHS recruitment website. A good standard of education and a full UK driving licence including C1 classification are required.

Qualified ambulance technicians start at £34,544 before tax. Trainees are paid £30,127, increasing as they progress through training. Experienced ambulance technicians can earn up to £43,039 per year before tax.

Ambulance technicians work with paramedics to deliver high-quality pre-hospital care, providing front-line responses to accidents and emergency incidents including 999 calls in very difficult and challenging environments.
Ambulance technicians work alongside qualified paramedics to deliver the highest possible level of care, managing a range of emergency situations with flexibility, compassion, and professionalism.
Useful school subjects include English, Biology, and Maths. A good standard of education and a full UK driving licence including C1 classification are required.
Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

Training is delivered through a 16-month internal Scottish Ambulance Service programme including 10 weeks of classroom training, a 4-week Certificate in Emergency Response Ambulance Driving, and placement at an ambulance station. Qualifications required include the Level 4 Diploma for Associate Ambulance Practitioners (SCQF level 7) and Certificate in Emergency Response Ambulance Driving (SCQF level 6).

Ambulance technicians can progress to become a paramedic by completing an undergraduate degree in Paramedic Science at Glasgow Caledonian University, Queen Margaret University, Robert Gordon University, University of Stirling, or University of the West of Scotland.
Ambulance technicians can voluntarily register with the UK Registry of Emergency Medical Technicians or the Health Practice Associates Council.
